name: Agent Run Ledger
description: A static AgentSite orchestration ledger for autonomous agent runs and verification evidence.
brief: Create a static GitHub Pages site that demonstrates an agent run ledger: a public-safe history of orchestrator decisions, delegated work, checks, deploy evidence, outcomes, failures, and next actions. It must not include raw private logs or claim live monitoring.
heroHeadline: A static ledger for autonomous AgentSite runs
heroLede: The page makes orchestrator and subagent work inspectable with curated run records, verification checks, deploy evidence, and next actions.
audience: Technical reviewers evaluating agent maintenance loops
visualDirection: dark cockpit orchestration dashboard with run history, filters, and verification evidence

sections.title: Runs become inspectable handoffs
sections.body: Each entry summarizes what happened, who delegated what, which checks passed, where it deployed, and what should happen next.

sections.title: Future agents can start from evidence
sections.body: The ledger gives later agents the context they need without scraping raw chat logs or guessing from commit history.

sections.title: Curated summaries only
sections.body: The ledger avoids raw transcripts, credentials, private logs, unsupported compliance claims, and live monitoring claims.

proofArtifacts.label: Run ledger data
proofArtifacts.body: src/data/runs.json contains public-safe run records validated by npm run check:runs.

proofArtifacts.label: Run ledger UI
proofArtifacts.body: src/components/AgentRunLedger.astro filters run records by text and status.

proofArtifacts.label: Run ledger QA
proofArtifacts.body: Visual QA exercises run-ledger search and status filters when the ledger is present.

allowedClaims: Repo-local QA scripts validate run records
approvalRequired: Adding analytics, cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party forms
Adding payment links or enabling payment mode
Indexing private logs, credentials, secrets, or unpublished user data
Adding live GitHub API monitoring, server runtime, authentication, or a database
